operations.problem: The explanation opens with 'Per 10-6-4c1' which is a paragraph citation instead of teaching the rule: when traffic prevents an unrestricted descent, ATC advises the aircraft it cannot approve the unrestricted descent, gives traffic, and requests intentions. The clearance text and choices are sound.
operations.source_basis: 10-6-4c1 states that when an aircraft requests an emergency descent, if traffic prevents an unrestricted descent ATC advises the aircraft of the traffic and requests its intentions. The prescribed phraseology is 'ATC ADVISES (aircraft identification) UNABLE TO APPROVE UNRESTRICTED DESCENT. TRAFFIC (traffic information). REQUEST INTENTIONS.'

content.instruction: The controller issued this transmission. Which pilot acknowledgment is correct?
content.clearance: ATC ADVISES N123AB UNABLE TO APPROVE UNRESTRICTED DESCENT. TRAFFIC TWELVE O'CLOCK, ONE FIVE MILES, OPPOSITE DIRECTION, FLIGHT LEVEL TWO NINER ZERO. REQUEST INTENTIONS.

choices.text: Unable unrestricted descent, traffic copied, requesting lower after traffic, N123AB.
Unrestricted descent approved, leaving flight level three five zero now, N123AB.
Traffic behind us, descending immediately to flight level two zero zero, N123AB.
No traffic factor, cancel emergency descent, N123AB.

content.explanation: Per 10-6-4c1, if traffic prevents an unrestricted descent, ATC advises the aircraft it cannot approve the unrestricted descent, gives traffic, and requests intentions.

content.instruction: The controller issued this transmission. Which pilot acknowledgment is correct?
content.clearance: ATC ADVISES N123AB UNABLE TO APPROVE UNRESTRICTED DESCENT. TRAFFIC TWELVE O'CLOCK, ONE FIVE MILES, OPPOSITE DIRECTION, FLIGHT LEVEL TWO NINER ZERO. REQUEST INTENTIONS.

choices.text: Unable unrestricted descent, traffic copied, requesting lower after traffic, N123AB.
Unrestricted descent approved, leaving flight level three five zero now, N123AB.
Traffic behind us, descending immediately to flight level two zero zero, N123AB.
No traffic factor, cancel emergency descent, N123AB.

content.explanation: When traffic prevents an unrestricted descent, ATC advises the aircraft it cannot approve the unrestricted descent, provides traffic information, and requests the pilot's intentions. The pilot should acknowledge the restriction, copy the traffic, and state their intentions.

operations.problem: The para_context opens with 'Under 10-6-4' which is paragraph-location scaffolding. The explanation references 'Per 10-6-4c2 through c6' which is a paragraph citation instead of teaching the immediate-action, broadcast, traffic-information, and clearance/instruction requirements for an unauthorized emergency descent.
operations.source_basis: 10-6-4c2 through c6 require that when an aircraft is making or will make an emergency descent without clearance, controllers must: advise other aircraft of the emergency descent using prescribed phraseology, take action immediately to safeguard all aircraft concerned, broadcast emergency information through available means when appropriate, provide traffic information if conditions permit, and issue appropriate clearances or instructions to all aircraft involved.

content.situation: An aircraft begins an emergency descent through other traffic in oceanic airspace without waiting for a clearance. Another controller wants to wait until the descent is complete before telling anyone else, because the descending aircraft is already committed and broadcasts would only create frequency congestion.
content.para_context: Under 10-6-4, take action immediately to safeguard all aircraft concerned. Advise other aircraft of the emergency descent, broadcast an emergency message to aircraft in the vicinity through available means, include the listed information, provide traffic information if conditions permit, and issue appropriate clearances or instructions as necessary. For ditching, the RCC determines the airspace to be blocked and the ACC blocks it until released.

choices.text: Act immediately to safeguard all aircraft, advise other aircraft of the emergency descent, broadcast the emergency information through available means, provide traffic information if possible, and issue necessary clearances or instructions.
Wait until the descent is complete before alerting nearby aircraft, because the emergency aircraft's maneuvering takes priority over all warnings.
Limit your action to the emergency aircraft only, because surrounding traffic is expected to remain clear on its own in oceanic airspace.
Issue no traffic or emergency broadcast unless the RCC first approves the message content.

content.explanation: Per 10-6-4c2 through c6, controllers immediately protect other aircraft when an emergency descent occurs, using traffic advisories, emergency broadcasts, and necessary clearances or instructions.

content.situation: An aircraft begins an emergency descent through other traffic in oceanic airspace without waiting for a clearance. Another controller wants to wait until the descent is complete before telling anyone else, because the descending aircraft is already committed and broadcasts would only create frequency congestion.
content.para_context: Controllers must take action immediately to safeguard all aircraft concerned when notified that an aircraft is making an emergency descent through other traffic. Advise other aircraft of the emergency descent using prescribed phraseology, broadcast an emergency message to aircraft in the vicinity through available means with required information, provide traffic information if conditions permit, and issue appropriate clearances or instructions as necessary.

choices.text: Act immediately to safeguard all aircraft, advise other aircraft of the emergency descent, broadcast the emergency information through available means, provide traffic information if possible, and issue necessary clearances or instructions.
Wait until the descent is complete before alerting nearby aircraft, because the emergency aircraft's maneuvering takes priority over all warnings.
Limit your action to the emergency aircraft only, because surrounding traffic is expected to remain clear on its own in oceanic airspace.
Issue no traffic or emergency broadcast unless the RCC first approves the message content.

content.explanation: Controllers must immediately protect other aircraft when an emergency descent occurs, using traffic advisories, emergency broadcasts with the required information elements, and necessary clearances or instructions. Waiting until the descent is complete would leave other aircraft unprotected during the most critical phase.

operations.problem: The explanation references 'Per 10-6-4c2' which is a paragraph citation instead of teaching that the emergency broadcast warns other aircraft of an emergency descent (not a climb) and stating the correct phraseology directly. The activity content — including original_phrase, display_text, tokens, error_index, and correct_token — is well-constructed and accurate.
operations.source_basis: 10-6-4c2(a) requires advising other aircraft of the emergency descent using the phraseology: 'ATC ADVISES (aircraft identification/all aircraft) BE ALERT FOR EMERGENCY DESCENT IN THE VICINITY OF (latitude/longitude) FROM (altitude/FL) TO (altitude/FL).'

content.instruction: One word in this phraseology is incorrect. Tap it.
content.original_phrase: ATC ADVISES ALL AIRCRAFT BE ALERT FOR EMERGENCY DESCENT IN THE VICINITY OF FOUR ZERO NORTH FIVE ZERO WEST FROM FLIGHT LEVEL THREE FIVE ZERO TO FLIGHT LEVEL ONE ZERO ZERO.
content.display_text: ATC ADVISES ALL AIRCRAFT BE ALERT FOR EMERGENCY CLIMB IN THE VICINITY OF FOUR ZERO NORTH FIVE ZERO WEST FROM FLIGHT LEVEL THREE FIVE ZERO TO FLIGHT LEVEL ONE ZERO ZERO
content.explanation: Per 10-6-4c2, the emergency broadcast warns other aircraft to be alert for an emergency descent in the vicinity.

content.original_phrase: ATC ADVISES ALL AIRCRAFT BE ALERT FOR EMERGENCY DESCENT IN THE VICINITY OF FOUR ZERO NORTH FIVE ZERO WEST FROM FLIGHT LEVEL THREE FIVE ZERO TO FLIGHT LEVEL ONE ZERO ZERO.
content.display_text: ATC ADVISES ALL AIRCRAFT BE ALERT FOR EMERGENCY CLIMB IN THE VICINITY OF FOUR ZERO NORTH FIVE ZERO WEST FROM FLIGHT LEVEL THREE FIVE ZERO TO FLIGHT LEVEL ONE ZERO ZERO
content.explanation: The emergency broadcast warns other aircraft to be alert for an emergency descent, not an emergency climb. The correct phraseology requires the word 'DESCENT' at this position to match the prescribed broadcast format.

operations.problem: The para_context opens with 'Under 10-6-5' which is paragraph-location scaffolding. The explanation references 'Per 10-6-5' which is a paragraph citation instead of teaching the fixed-clearance-limit practice and the two-condition requirement for discontinuing IFR separation.
operations.source_basis: 10-6-5a requires IFR separation between the SAR aircraft and the aircraft in distress unless visual or radar contact has been established by the SAR aircraft and both pilots concur. 10-6-5b requires clearing the SAR aircraft to a fixed clearance limit rather than to the moving distressed aircraft, with route clearances consistent with the distressed aircraft.

content.situation: A SAR aircraft is trying to assist a distressed aircraft in oceanic airspace. Another controller wants to clear the SAR aircraft directly to the distressed aircraft as the clearance limit and continue full IFR separation even after the SAR crew has visual contact and both crews agree they can work visually.
content.para_context: Under 10-6-5, provide IFR separation between the SAR aircraft and the aircraft in distress unless visual or radar contact has been established by the search and rescue aircraft and both pilots concur, in which case IFR separation may be discontinued. Clear the SAR aircraft to a fixed clearance limit rather than to the moving distressed aircraft, issue route clearances consistent with the distressed aircraft, advise the rescue aircraft of adverse factors, and keep agencies, operator, and aircraft informed.

choices.text: Clear the SAR aircraft to a fixed clearance limit, keep its route consistent with the distressed aircraft, and discontinue IFR separation only if the SAR aircraft has visual or radar contact and both pilots concur.
Clear the SAR aircraft directly to the distressed aircraft as the moving clearance limit, because rescue missions are exempt from normal clearance-limit rules.
Maintain IFR separation in every case even after visual or radar contact and pilot concurrence, because rescue operations may never discontinue IFR separation.
Ignore route consistency with the distressed aircraft, because only the RCC determines how the SAR aircraft should navigate.

content.explanation: Per 10-6-5, the SAR aircraft is cleared to a fixed clearance limit and IFR separation may be discontinued only when the stated contact-and-concurrence conditions are met.

Ignore route consistency with the distressed aircraft, because only the RCC determines how the SAR aircraft should navigate.

content.explanation: The SAR aircraft is cleared to a fixed clearance limit, not to the moving distressed aircraft. IFR separation may be discontinued only when visual or radar contact is established by the SAR aircraft and both pilots concur.

operations.problem: The stem begins with 'Under 10-7-1' and the explanation cites 'Per 10-7-1c', substituting paragraph-number trivia for operational substance. The student should recognize this as a terminal-environment procedure without needing the paragraph label.

replacements.question_text: In a terminal environment, what must be done with information concerning a ground missile emergency?
replacements.explanation: Terminal facilities must relay all information concerning a ground missile emergency to the ARTCC within whose area the emergency exists and disseminate it as a NOTAM.

choices.text: Relay all information to the ARTCC within whose area the emergency exists and disseminate it as a NOTAM.
Hold the information locally until after the emergency ends, then include it in the daily summary log.

operations.source_basis: 10-7-1a: 'Air carrier company radio stations for each VFR company aircraft which is or will be operating in the vicinity of the emergency.' 10-7-1b: 'FSSs adjacent to the emergency location.'

replacements.question_text: In an en route environment, which facilities are used to issue alerting advisories for a ground missile emergency?
replacements.explanation: En route facilities must use air carrier company radio stations for each VFR company aircraft operating in the vicinity of the emergency and FSSs adjacent to the emergency location to issue alerting advisories.

choices.text: Air carrier company radio stations for VFR aircraft in the vicinity and adjacent FSSs.
Only adjacent control towers and the TRACONs that serve them.
Only the nearest Rescue Coordination Center and airport management office.

operations.source_basis: 10-7-1: 'When you receive information concerning a ground missile emergency, notify other concerned facilities and take action to have alerting advisories issued by: a. EN ROUTE. Air carrier company radio stations... b. EN ROUTE. FSSs adjacent... c. TERMINAL. Relay all information... to the ARTCC... and disseminate as a NOTAM.'

content.situation: You receive word of a ground missile emergency affecting a mixed en route and terminal environment. Another controller wants to notify only the local tower and let each adjacent facility discover the rest from the NOTAM once it is published.
content.para_context: Under 10-7-1, notify other concerned facilities and take action to have alerting advisories issued by air carrier company radio stations for VFR company aircraft in the vicinity, by adjacent FSSs, and in terminal environments relay all information to the ARTCC within whose area the emergency exists and disseminate it as a NOTAM.

choices.text: Notify the other concerned facilities and ensure the listed en route and terminal alerting actions occur, including company radio, adjacent FSS, ARTCC relay, and NOTAM dissemination as applicable.
Notify only the local tower because the NOTAM system will automatically alert company radio stations, adjacent FSSs, and the ARTCC without further ATC action.
Limit the notification to IFR aircraft because VFR company aircraft are outside the required alerting advisory chain.
Wait until the emergency is over before relaying it to the ARTCC, because missile-emergency information is handled only after confirmation of impact area size.

Notify only the local tower because the NOTAM system will automatically alert company radio stations, adjacent FSSs, and the ARTCC without further ATC action.
Limit the notification to IFR aircraft because VFR company aircraft are outside the required alerting advisory chain.

operations.source_basis: 10-7-1 requires notification via air carrier company radio stations for VFR company aircraft (en route), adjacent FSSs (en route), and ARTCC relay with NOTAM dissemination (terminal).

match.front: What must ATC do when it receives information about a ground missile emergency?
match.back: Notify other concerned facilities and ensure alerting advisories are issued by the appropriate stations or facilities.

replacements.front: What must ATC do when it receives information about a ground missile emergency?
replacements.back: Notify other concerned facilities and take action to have alerting advisories issued — en route via air carrier company radio stations for VFR aircraft and adjacent FSSs; terminal via ARTCC relay and NOTAM dissemination.

An aircraft under radar control and the emergency location which can be accurately determined by reference to the radar scope. 2. The airspace to be protected for the route being flown.'

replacements.question_text: What lateral separation minimum is used to reroute IFR or SVFR aircraft around a ground missile emergency when the emergency location can be accurately determined on radar?
replacements.explanation: The lateral separation minimum for rerouting IFR and SVFR aircraft around a ground missile emergency is 1 mile between the emergency location and the aircraft under radar control, or between the emergency location and the airspace to be protected for the route being flown.

choices.text: 1 mile lateral.
500 feet lateral.
3 miles lateral in every case.
No fixed lateral minimum is provided.

replacements.explanation: The vertical separation minimum for rerouting IFR and SVFR aircraft to avoid a ground missile emergency is 6,000 feet above the surface over the emergency location.

choices.text: 6,000 feet above the surface over the emergency location.
3,000 feet above the surface over the emergency location.
10,000 feet MSL regardless of terrain.

replacements.question_text: When rerouting IFR or SVFR aircraft around a ground missile emergency, what should a controller do if the notifying official suggests greater separation minima than the standard 1 mile lateral or 6,000 feet vertical?
replacements.explanation: Controllers must reroute IFR and SVFR aircraft to avoid the emergency location by the listed minima or by greater minima when suggested by the notifying official. The notifying official may direct larger separation distances, and the controller must apply them.
